source.definition: A covered or open passage, often in museums or buildings, used for displaying artworks; also a room or building where art is exhibited.

explanation.sourceText: The English word "gallery" comes from the French "galerie", which in turn derives from the Italian "galleria", originating from the Late Latin "galeria", a term for a covered walkway or colonnade.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „gallery” provine din francezul „galerie”, care la rândul său derivă din italianul „galleria”, provenind din latină târzie „galeria”, termen pentru un coridor acoperit sau un portic cu coloane.

target.definition: Un spațiu acoperit sau deschis, de obicei într-un muzeu sau clădire, destinat expunerii operelor de artă; de asemenea, o încăpere sau clădire în care sunt expuse opere de artă.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word "galerie" was directly borrowed from the French "galerie", which itself comes from the Italian "galleria" and the Late Latin "galeria".
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „galerie” este un împrumut direct din francezul „galerie”, provenit la rândul său din italianul „galleria” și din latină târzie „galeria”.

examples.sourceText: The gallery displays modern art from around the world.
examples.targetText: Galeria expune artă modernă din întreaga lume.

examples.sourceText: We visited the gallery in the city center.
examples.targetText: Am vizitat galeria din centrul orașului.

examples.sourceText: Many famous paintings hang in the gallery.
examples.targetText: În galerie sunt expuse multe picturi celebre.

examples.sourceText: The gallery has a long, covered corridor.
examples.targetText: Galeria are un coridor lung, acoperit.

learningTips.text: The English word "gallery" sounds very similar to the Romanian "galerie" and both refer to places where art is displayed, making it easy to remember.
Think of a famous art gallery like the National Gallery in London to connect the word with a real-world example.
Imagine walking through a long corridor lined with paintings and sculptures — this image helps link the meaning of "gallery" with the Romanian "galerie".
Remember that "gallery" comes from French and Italian roots, just like many English art-related words, which can help you recall its meaning and spelling.
